column5: We built a new location page targeting Electricians Wirral, covering the whole Wirral peninsula - Birkenhead, Wallasey, Bebington, Heswall, West Kirby, Hoylake and Bromborough - across the CH41 to CH49 and CH60 to CH63 postcodes. The page has unique local copy for commercial, industrial and domestic work, NICEIC and OZEV trust signals, LocalBusiness and FAQ schema, and it pulls in our documented Prenton, Wirral distribution project as real proof of local work. Wirral is a large Merseyside market right on our doorstep with strong domestic and commercial search demand, and until now we only picked it up indirectly through the Ellesmere Port page. Page submitted to Google for indexing. Keyword being tracked: electricians Wirral.

We added the new Wirral page to the XML sitemap, along with the seven existing location pages that were missing from it, so all area pages can now be discovered and indexed. We also cross-linked every area page to the new Wirral page and made Wirral clickable on the Areas We Cover page, so the new page inherits internal link equity from the rest of the site. Sitemap resubmitted in Search Console.

Wirral is one of the larger local markets we cover, and demand looks healthy going into the second half of the year. The ongoing Wirral Waters regeneration around Birkenhead docks and steady growth in EV charger and solar work across Merseyside are keeping search interest up for local electricians, EICRs and EV charger installs. Having a dedicated Wirral page means we can capture that demand directly rather than leaning on the neighbouring Ellesmere Port page.

We updated the website navigation menu to include a dropdown menu under Services, grouping Commercial, Industrial, and Domestic service links together. We also added a link to the Home page to make it easier for visitors to navigate the site.

We built a new service page dedicated to industrial electrical installations, detailing our capabilities in three-phase power distribution, PLC control systems, and machinery wiring to attract local plant managers.
We created a new service page focusing on domestic electrical work, covering rewires, consumer unit upgrades, and smart home installations to help local homeowners find certified contractors.

We updated the commercial electrical services page with new local imagery and expanded details on office and retail fit-outs, fire safety compliance, and out-of-hours scheduling to build trust with local business clients.

We fixed a layout conflict in the stylesheet that was forcing dark-themed content sections to render with a white background, which made white text invisible and created large blank gaps across the website.
We updated both the XML and HTML sitemaps to include the new industrial and domestic electrical services pages, ensuring search engines can index these pages and human users can discover them.

We updated the services.html index page and home.html page to link to the newly created location-neutral industrial.html and domestic.html services pages, replacing links that previously pointed to the contact page or fragment sections.

We updated the TrustBar component to dynamically accept the local area dataset on all location pages, ensuring that the pin location icon shows the actual target area (e.g. 'Wirral · CH41', 'Wrexham · LL11') instead of hardcoding 'Chester · CH3' across the whole site.

We refactored locations.jsx and generate-seo.js to reduce excessive location name density in headings, lede copy, paragraph text, and landmarks across all local area pages, making the content read far more naturally for human visitors while maintaining key SEO heading structures.

We removed the confusing 'Distance from HQ' fact card from the homepage and all location pages, balancing the layout across 4 cards. We also styled the services grid on location pages (.locations-services-grid) to stay on a single row on desktop screen widths (>1300px) and wrap responsively to 3, 2, or 1 column on tablet and mobile viewports.

We implemented a multi-step contact/quote enquiry form as a modal popup triggered by CTA buttons on all local area coverage pages. The modal features scroll prevention, backdrop blur, an Escape-key close listener, and a large close button, making it fully optimized and responsive for mobile users.
